content.text: The cryptocurrency market includes various assets with different price points. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is currently valued at $80,488.74, reflecting its close connection to Bitcoin. Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) is priced at $2,018.31, maintaining its role as a tokenized version of Ethereum. Solana (SOL) is trading at $126.42, continuing its presence as a major blockchain platform. Meanwhile, ai16z, a lesser-known asset, is priced at $0.1925.
A fix was implemented to resolve a missing moment rollup external issue in the-org build.
Core types were added to enable the generation of index.d.ts in the /dist directory, addressing errors related to missing types in imports.

A user reported being unable to get past the pnpm install and build process due to an issue with switching pnpm to version 9.15.7. Despite forcing the version switch, build commands failed due to missing pnpm CLI errors.

categories.title: NEAR AI Image Generation Fix & AI Agent Enhancements

content.text: A fix was implemented for NEAR AI's image generation API, addressing an issue where it defaulted to OpenAI. The update ensures proper invocation of NEAR AI's image generation, with testing confirming its functionality. The fix involved adding a missing NEAR AI branch in the `generateImage` function, using OpenAI-compatible APIs.

Discussions on AI hallucinations and fact-checking systems led to proposals for real-time data validation and confidence thresholds for AI-generated responses. These enhancements aim to improve AI accuracy, particularly for trading bots. Additionally, DegenAI (now Spartan) is being integrated into ElizaOS v2, enabling trading, LP management, and intel functionalities within Discord and Telegram. The migration to ElizaOS v2 caused delays due to code consolidation into a mono repo, with ongoing bug fixes.

A newsletter workflow was introduced, aggregating daily news, summarizing it, and processing it into a weekly newsletter for peer review before publishing. HackMD is used for collaboration, with features for comments and suggestions. Additionally, a hackathon concept, 'The AIpprentice,' was proposed, where AI agents compete to be the most useful intern.

categories.title: Technical Discussions and Troubleshooting in Discord

content.text: The Discord plugin auto-initializes if `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` and `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID` are set in `.env`. Required permissions include `Presence Intent`, `Server Members Intent`, and `Message Content Intent`.
A MongoDB sharding issue was resolved by switching from a standalone instance to a replica set or `mongos` router. Free-tier MongoDB Atlas does not support sharding.

A blank UI issue in the Eliza client was debugged by checking browser console errors, clearing cache, verifying `VITE_API_URL`, and ensuring the correct Node.js version (`v23.3.0`). Running `pnpm run dev:client` helped diagnose the issue.

To disable auto-posting in the Twitter agent, set `TWITTER_AUTO_TWEET=false`, `TWITTER_AUTO_LIKE=false`, and `TWITTER_AUTO_RETWEET=false`. Common issues included Twitter API rate limits and Cloudflare blocks.
A new plugin was developed to generate text and save it to a file. Key steps included defining an action, using `fs.writeFileSync`, and registering the plugin in `character.json`.
ElizaOS plugins must be explicitly registered using `npx elizaos plugins add @elizaos-plugins/client-twitter` or similar.

Discord client debugging involved checking for missing intents, incorrect `.env` variables, and outdated dependencies. Running `DEBUG=eliza:discord*` and reinstalling `discord.js@14.x` helped resolve issues.

Twitter scraper issues, such as `scraper.getFollowers()` returning 404 errors, were caused by rate limits, expired authentication, or private accounts. Solutions included using fresh cookies, implementing request delays, and rotating accounts.
Switching from DeepSeek to Llama AI models caused unexpected behavior. Suggested solutions included using an instruction-tuned model (`llama-3.2-3b-instruct`) and verifying model configurations.

categories.title: Issues and Fixes in ElizaOS Plugins

content.text: A user reported an issue with the '@elizaos-plugins/client-twitter' plugin, which fails to process Twitter actions despite all other plugins working correctly. The issue persists regardless of whether 'ragKnowledge' is enabled or disabled. Logs indicate deprecation warnings and an error related to the missing '@elizaos-plugins/client-telegram' package. The system successfully initializes and logs in but encounters errors when processing tweets.

A fix was introduced for the '@elizaos-plugins/client-telegram' plugin, adding a 'serviceType' property to support multi-platform functionality for Ruby. This minor change allows for cross-platform testing.
Concerns were raised about the small liquidity pool for ai16z. Liquidity is currently spread across Raydium pools, and Wintermute is market-making with 30M+ tokens.
The project team is actively working on multiple initiatives: Shaw is developing v2, while other members focus on DegenAI, a launchpad, and new tokenomics.
A game project team has shifted focus to token integration, moving up their original Q3 timeline for testing.
A JSON feed for AI news updates encountered a 404 error but was later resolved. There was also a suggestion to automate the news update process in a loop.
Consideration was given to renting a GPU for hosting the news update process, but the cost-effectiveness was uncertain.
A 1.8GB behind-the-scenes video for Demo Day was shared, with a suggestion to upload it to YouTube for easier access.
A fix was implemented for missing moment rollup external in the ElizaOS build.
Core types were added to enable index.d.ts to be built in /dist, resolving missing type errors on imports.
A user reported being unable to get past `pnpm install` and build due to a missing pnpm CLI error. The issue affected multiple ElizaOS packages.

A tutorial on building a Social AI Agent in 15 minutes was found to be outdated, with discrepancies between the video and textual instructions. The Twitter plugin integration method has changed, requiring updates to the documentation.

A user reported an 'Error processing tweet undefined' issue when using multiple ElizaOS plugins, including Twitter, web search, and image generation. The error persisted despite the fork being up to date.
A fix was implemented for NEAR AI image generation, ensuring the correct API invocation instead of falling back to OpenAI.
A minor fix was made to the Telegram plugin, adding `serviceType` to support multi-platform testing for Ruby.
Six contributors merged two PRs, focusing on various improvements.
